title: Nia CLI Codebase Tour
description: A guided walkthrough of the Nia CLI — a multi-source code search and autonomous research tool built with TypeScript, Bun, and Crust JS

steps.title: Welcome to Nia CLI
steps.description: Nia CLI is a command-line interface for the Nia platform — a multi-source code search and research tool with autonomous research capabilities. The codebase is written in TypeScript, runs on Bun, and uses the Crust JS framework for CLI construction. Key areas: src/ for application code, tests/ for the test suite, and configuration files at the root.

interactiveActions.description: Hover over the src/ directory to see the application code

steps.title: CLI Entry Points
steps.description: The cli starts at src/cli.ts, which registers all 13+ commands as subcommands and sets up plugins (update notifier, version, help, auto-complete and skill integration for LLM usage). Global flags like api-key, verbose, and color are defined in src/app.ts — separated to prevent circular imports between commands.

interactiveActions.description: Click cli.ts to see how commands and plugins are registered
Click app.ts to see the global flag definitions

steps.title: Command Modules
steps.description: The commands/ directory contains 14 command modules, each following a consistent pattern: subcommands via .sub(), skill annotations for LLM integration, error-wrapped handlers via withErrorHandling(), and output formatting via OutputRenderer. Key commands include search (universal/targeted/web/deep research), oracle (autonomous research jobs), sources (documentation management), repos (GitHub indexing), local (folder sync), and contexts (memory sessions).

interactiveActions.description: Click search.ts to see the universal search command with multiple modes
Click oracle.ts to explore autonomous research streaming

steps.title: Services Layer
steps.description: The services/ directory provides core infrastructure. config.ts manages API key resolution through a chain: CLI --api-key flag → NIA_API_KEY env var → config/nia/config.json. sdk.ts is a factory that creates configured NiaSDK instances. The local/ subdirectory is the most complex service — handling local folder synchronization with file watching, content extraction, batched uploads, and retry logic.

interactiveActions.description: Click config.ts to see the API key resolution chain
Hover over local/ to see the sync service modules

steps.title: Local Folder Sync (Deep Dive)
steps.description: The local sync system is the most complex service in the CLI. It consists of 5 modules: types.ts (data models), watcher.ts (chokidar file system watcher with 2s debounce), extractor.ts (incremental folder scanning with cursor-based resumption, 10MB file limit, 76+ supported extensions), sync.ts (orchestration with 500-file/4MB batching and exponential backoff retries), and api.ts (low-level HTTP calls to the Nia daemon API).

highlightLayers.name: Data Flow: Types → Extract → Sync → API

interactiveActions.description: Click sync.ts to see batching and retry logic
Click extractor.ts to see the file scanning and skip lists

steps.title: Utility Modules
steps.description: Three utility modules support the entire CLI. output.ts provides OutputRenderer with 3 formats (JSON, table, text) and TTY-aware, status-colored rendering. errors.ts centralizes error handling — mapping SDK, API, and HTTP errors to user-friendly messages with verbose debug mode. streaming.ts renders SSE streams with event-type labeling (thinking, searching, reading, analyzing) for the Oracle's autonomous research output.

interactiveActions.description: Click output.ts to see the flexible rendering system
Click streaming.ts to see SSE event rendering

steps.title: Testing & CI/CD
steps.description: The tests/ directory contains 19 test files using Bun's native test runner, covering commands, utilities, and configuration. CI runs on pull requests (lint → type check → test → build) and releases are managed via Changesets with npm publishing. To get started: run 'bun install', then 'bun run dev' for development, 'bun run test' for tests, and 'bun run check' for linting.

interactiveActions.description: Hover over tests/ to see the full test suite
Explore the full codebase — you're ready to contribute!
